root_cause: EffectComposer stores physical pixel dimensions from custom render targets directly into _width/_height fields, but setSize() treats these as logical pixels and multiplies by pixel ratio, causing double-scaling on resize.

EffectComposer.setSize() uses a cached _pixelRatio from construction time instead of querying the current renderer pixel ratio, causing render targets to be sized incorrectly when device pixel ratio changes during window resize.

offending_symbol: EffectComposer constructor (lines 74-75) and lack of automatic resize tracking; also missing automatic sync with renderer.size
root_cause: EffectComposer render targets are not automatically resized when the browser window resizes. They retain their initial dimensions (200x150) unless the application explicitly calls composer.setSize(width, height), causing the effects scene to render at low resolution and be upscaled.

EffectComposer.setSize() does not re-fetch the current pixel ratio from the renderer before calculating render target dimensions, causing it to use a stale cached _pixelRatio value and size buffers incorrectly.

The Gaussian blur shader in _getSeparableBlurMaterial hardcodes alpha to 1.0, destroying transparency during blur passes.

Window-space depth in perspective projection is non-linear (1/z function). With tight near/far ratio (550-650) and distant camera position, objects compress into the last 30% of depth range then squeeze again due to hyperbolic 1/z term, making nearly all visible objects map to identical depth values

PointsMaterial inherits depthWrite: true from Material, causing transparent point sprites to write depth values that block other transparent sprites behind them from rendering.

three.js does not automatically disable depth writing when transparent:true is set, causing overlapping transparent point sprites to fail depth test and show background instead of blending properly.

DoubleSide transparent objects with NormalBlending render both front and back faces in a single pass, causing overlapping semi-transparent surfaces to accumulate color through additive blending, which produces visual artifacts. SubtractiveBlending avoids this because its non-additive blend function

Double-sided transparent objects use per-object depth sorting with a single z-value, but render in two passes (back faces, then front faces). Interior objects sort incorrectly because they're compared to the transparent object's center rather than its individual face layers.

FilterSystem renders to filter textures with autoGenerateMipmaps enabled but never calls updateMipmaps() to regenerate mipmap content after rendering, causing filters that do scaled UV sampling to read uninitialized/corrupt mipmap data.

Back-face culling is enabled by default (material.side defaults to FrontSide). When nested cylinders are rotated to angles where their back-faces face the camera, WebGL culls those faces away, making the cylinders disappear.

User set opacity < 1 but did not set transparent: true on the material, causing three.js to disable GL blending (WebGLState.js:776-778 checks both conditions).

_colorMatrix method incorrectly normalized offset values by dividing by 255 only when multiply=true, treating already-normalized 0-1 values as if they were in 0-255 range, causing offset to become ~255x smaller than intended.

The V_GGX_SmithCorrelated_Anisotropic function lacks epsilon protection in its denominator, allowing division by very small numbers that produce NaN/Inf artifacts. The isotropic version uses max(denominator, EPSILON) but the anisotropic version omits this guard.

The D_GGX_Anisotropic function (line 117 in lights_physical_pars_fragment.glsl.js) divides by v2 without epsilon protection. When dotTH and dotBH are near zero (anisotropy axes perpendicular to half-direction), v2 approaches zero, causing w2 to become NaN, which renders as black pixels.
Unguarded division by v2 in D_GGX_Anisotropic function causes NaN/infinity when v2 approaches zero at specific light/view angles with anisotropic materials.

The TriangleIndexArray uses uint16 (Uint16Array) to store mesh indices, which can only represent values 0-65535. When a GLB model with > 65,536 vertices is loaded, indices >= 65536 silently truncate to (value & 0xFFFF), causing upper vertices to address lower vertex data.
Default minFilter is gl.NEAREST instead of a mipmap filter, preventing mipmap generation for GLB textures without explicit samplers.

expandedFarZProjMatrix uses a limited far clipping plane for non-globe projections, whereas it should use an infinite (or very large) far plane like globe does. GLB model vertices are clipped by this limited far plane and don't render.
The setDepthMode condition uses OR (||) instead of AND (&&), incorrectly disabling depth testing when depth mask is ReadOnly, even though depth function requires testing (LEQUAL).
